notes: Hand-authored ground truth mirrors frontend/src/data/plays/black.ts. svg_tolerance of 3.0 accounts for homography drift observed on page-60 registration (P2/P3 drift ~5 units in y, P1/P4/P5 drift <2.5 units).

BLOB 1-4 high set. Panel 0 covers book Instructions 1-2: '5 sprints to strong side corner. 3 sprints to short corner. 2 enters the ball in-bounds to 5 in the corner.' Movement endpoints MEASURED via src/cv/measure.py from pixel arrow-tips on results/cv/page_015_panel_0_source.png — NOT guessed. P1 arrow is visible in the panel but book prose does not describe P1 moving during Instructions 1-2; included at tolerance=4.0 to absorb pixel-read imprecision (±5 px → ±0.3 SVG units) plus arrow-tip ambiguity.

Panel 1 covers book Instruction 3: '5 skip passes the ball opposite to 4.' Player positions from results/cv/page_015_report.txt panel 1. Only the single skip pass is ground-truthed; CV detection does not need spatial endpoint since to_player is sufficient.

Panel 2 covers book Instructions 4-7. The only unambiguous movement is the final skip pass from book Instruction 7: '4 skips the basketball to 2.' Flare screens (3 on x2, 5 on x3), cuts (1 spacing up, 2 sprinting), and the final cut are all visible in the panel but require pixel-endpoint measurement and screen-bar-vs-cut disambiguation that I have not completed — not committed to avoid hallucinated coordinates.

1-3-1 set. Panel 0 covers book Instruction 1: '1 passing to 3 on the wing.' Positions from results/cv/page_050_report.txt panel 0. The zigzag-then-dashed visible from P1 may encode a short dribble followed by the pass — the book treats it as a single Instruction-1 pass. Committed only the pass to avoid ambiguous dribble-vs-pass scoring.

Panel 1 covers book Instruction 4: '3 makes the strong skip pass to 2 for the shot.' P5 screen on D4 (Instruction 2) and P2 slide to corner (Instruction 3) are visible but not ground-truthed here — the skip pass is the unambiguous primary action. Positions from panel 1 report.

1-4 high set. Panel 0 covers book Instructions 1-3. P2 and P3 cross sides (confirmed by position delta between panel 0 and panel 1 in the CV report: P2 moves from x=-19 to x=+18, P3 from x=+19 to x=-21). Book Instruction 3: '1 makes the pass to 2 as they come off of 4's screen.' The pass receiver is P2 AT ITS NEW POSITION (post-exchange), which is on the right wing. CV without multi-panel state tracking will likely mis-attribute to P3 (original right-wing player) — this case will score low until state tracking is added.

High 2-3 formation. Panel 0 covers book Instructions 1-2: '1 passes to 3' plus '1 and 2 cut off 5 towards the opposite blocks.' Only the pass is ground-truthed; the two cuts are visible and would require pixel measurement of each arrow tip (curves crossing at the paint) — skipped for now to avoid hallucinated endpoints. Positions from panel 0 report.
